title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Chapter 1 concepts!

questions.question: What is C++?
options: An extension of the C programming language
questions.explanation: C++ was created by Bjarne Stroustrup as an extension of the C language.

questions.question: C++ code is run directly by the browser.
questions.explanation: C++ code must be compiled into machine-readable code before it can run.

questions.question: Which of these correctly prints text to the screen in C++?
questions.explanation: The correct syntax for outputting text in C++ is std::cout <<.

questions.question: Every C++ statement must end with a semicolon (;).
questions.explanation: Semicolons mark the end of a statement in C++.

questions.question: What does the line #include <iostream> do?
options: It imports tools needed for input/output, like printing to the screen.
questions.explanation: iostream stands for input-output stream, providing the std::cout tool.

questions.question: The starting point of every C++ program is the _____ function.
questions.explanation: Any code inside int main() will be executed when the program runs.

questions.question: What is the purpose of return 0; in the main function?
options: To indicate that the program ended successfully
To print a zero on the screen
questions.explanation: return 0 tells the operating system everything went well.

questions.question: To write a single-line comment, use _____.
questions.explanation: // ignores everything after it on the same line.

questions.question: How do you end a multi-line comment?
questions.explanation: Multi-line comments open with /* and close with */.

questions.question: The compiler ignores comments in the code.
questions.explanation: Comments are strictly for human reading, the compiler ignores them completely.

questions.question: Who is the creator of C++?
questions.explanation: Bjarne Stroustrup developed C++.

questions.question: If you use `using namespace std;`, what can you write instead of `std::cout`?
questions.explanation: It allows you to omit the `std::` prefix.

questions.question: What is the insertion operator in C++ used with cout?
questions.explanation: << is the insertion operator.

questions.question: C++ is often used for high-performance applications like video game engines.
questions.explanation: Because it is compiled, C++ is extremely fast.

questions.question: What happens if you forget a semicolon in C++?
options: The compiler will print a warning but run the code.
The program will not compile and throw an error.
questions.explanation: Semicolons are mandatory. Forgetting one will cause a syntax error.

questions.question: The `{` and `}` symbols are called _____ brackets.
questions.explanation: They define blocks of code, like the body of the main function.

questions.question: Which software commonly uses C++ internally?
questions.explanation: C++ is used in all of these systems.

questions.question: To compile C++ means to translate it into machine code.
questions.explanation: Compilers translate human-readable code into machine-executable instructions.

questions.question: Which operator provides input/output stream capabilities?
questions.explanation: The header is <iostream>.

questions.question: In C++, code is executed top-to-bottom sequentially.
questions.explanation: The compiler reads and executes statements systematically from top to bottom.
